## [00:00 - 03:04]

What are the most common advertising mistakes related to placements? We're going to cover that today. You can read my blog post on this as well at JohnLumer.com slash placements. So first, let's set the scene a bit. At the ad set level, we have the option of manually changing placements. What is the rate of people clicking? The click through rate. But the reality was it was so cheap to reach people in that placement that it was still worthwhile. Now back then when advertisers were removing these placements and just focusing on news feeds as an example, they didn't necessarily suffer the consequences because it was so cheap to reach people. But fast forward, it's way more expensive now. So there may be times where you are actually hurting yourself by removing some of these placements. type of conversion, you should consider removing placements. If you're going to optimize for anything top of the funnel, so especially link clicks, landing page views, also seen it in through play. ## [06:07 - 08:56]

seconds of your video, if not to the end. I was like, okay, is that a penny cost per through play? This is barely any budget spent on it because then I figured out what was happening. I broke down, if you're not familiar with how you find these results by placement, click this drop down menu, you might even have recently used a popular placement, but otherwise, it's going to be under delivery and select placement, it'll create a different row for each placement. 654 of the 665 through plays came from audience network rewarded video. So what is it about that placement that I might be concerned about? Let's see how meta defines it. rewarded video ads are a full screen experience where users opt in to view a video ad in exchange for something of value such as virtual currency in app items, exclusive content and more. So basically, when someone sees this ad, it's not that they're super excited about your ad want to watch to the end. It's they're actually in the middle of playing some game on their phone that is monetized with audience network rewarded video, and in order to get some virtual currency or something else in that game, they need to watch this video. I think we've all been there we've seen apps that do that. They don't care about your video and what you'll see is these people won't do anything else. They don't click. So sure, maybe it's fine for awareness, but if you're hoping that they were performing another action, they won't do it in most cases. So bottom line when it comes to placements, if you're optimizing for any type of conversion, use advanced plus placements where it gives the algorithm the ability to access any of the placements, it may not use them all it may not use audience network, etc. But at least allow that possibility in case maybe you can get some conversions from that. Otherwise, if you're optimizing for anything top the funnel, so that's link clicks landing page views, probably post engagement through place, be very careful. I mean, it's questionable whether you should be optimizing for those kinds of actions anyway, because of quality concerns, but placements will make it even worse. Because the algorithm doesn't care whether these people do anything else. It just thinks you want this action, we're going to get you as many of this thing as possible.
